operating_state: Net sales increased 5% to $9.6 billion driven by price realization across all segments, partially offset by currency headwinds and lower shipment volumes. Equipment operations achieved 7% price realization while financial services revenues declined reflecting lower average portfolio balances. Consolidated net income fell to $903 million from $1.2 billion as cost inflation outpaced pricing actions.
cost_structure: Cost of sales ratio deteriorated to 78.5% from 72.1% driven by elevated production costs from UAW work stoppage impacts, supply chain disruptions, and material cost inflation. Operating profit declined 32% with research and development expenses increasing while other operating expenses decreased driven by reduced equipment lease depreciation and lower retirement benefit costs.
material_events: UAW ratification bonus of $90 million expensed following six-year collective bargaining agreement with 10% immediate wage increase covering U.S. facilities. Acquired majority ownership in Kreisel Electric battery technology provider. Share repurchases totaled $623 million with quarterly dividend declared at $1.05 per share. Completed voluntary contribution to U.S. OPEB plan and announced Hitachi joint venture dissolution.
macro_exposures: Supply chain disruptions and global logistics constraints continue impacting production costs and delivery commitments with currency translation reducing sales. Labor market tightness and increased turnover from pandemic effects creating operational challenges. Commodity price volatility and geopolitical tensions affecting input costs with new exposure to Russian administrative requirements.
forward_outlook: Industry sales forecasted higher across regions with large agricultural machinery growth expected in North America and construction equipment growth domestically. Management expects continued material cost pressures and supply chain challenges through fiscal 2022. Financial services net income expected slightly lower due to higher credit loss provisions partially offset by higher portfolio income.
uncertainty_profile: Pandemic duration and variant impacts remain uncertain affecting operations, supply chains, and customer demand with ongoing supply chain volatility and material cost inflation. Pension plan remeasurement due to UAW collective bargaining agreement decreased funded status and will increase annual pension expense. Pending legal proceedings and cybersecurity risks represent operational uncertainties.
